factors.summary: The company is facing material litigation with potential significant financial impact.

evidence.text: On June 24, 2019, FedEx filed suit in U.S. District Court in the District of Columbia seeking to enjoin the U.S. Department of Commerce (the 'DOC') from enforcing prohibitions contained in the Export Administration Regulations against FedEx. On November 5, 2020, we appealed this decision.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are not a significant factor in the company's current financial position.

evidence.text: Historically, we have been successful in obtaining unsecured financing from both domestic and international sources, although the marketplace for such investment capital can become restricted depending on a variety of economic factors.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, driven by higher net income and lower pension contributions.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operating activities increased $3.2 billion in the first half of 2021 primarily due to higher net income and lower pension contributions.

factors.summary: Brexit poses significant risks to the company's operations, particularly in Europe and the UK, with potential for market disruption and increased regulatory costs.

evidence.text: The United Kingdom’s withdrawal from the European Union (“Brexit”) could adversely impact our business, results of operations and financial condition. [...] Any additional impact of Brexit will depend on the terms of such resolution, if one is ultimately reached. Even if the United Kingdom maintains access to the EU single market and trade deals following the transition period, Brexit could result in further economic downturn globally.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin international services, particularly in Europe, as part of the TNT Express integration.

evidence.text: We expect the integration of the FedEx Express and TNT Express linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations to begin offering an enhanced portfolio of international services in 2021. We expect to complete the final phase of international air network interoperability in early calendar 2022.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are e-commerce and international trade, particularly in Asia-Pacific and Europe.

evidence.text: The demand for our U.S. domestic residential service offerings continued to accelerate during the second quarter of 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. International export package average daily volumes increased 19% in the second quarter and 17% in the first half of 2021 led by volume growth from Asia-Pacific and Europe.

factors.summary: Completion of TNT Express integration and new pricing initiatives are expected to be significant catalysts for future growth.

evidence.text: We expect to complete the final phase of international air network interoperability in early calendar 2022. These actions are focused on reducing the complexity and fragmentation of our international business, improving efficiency to meet changing customer expectations and business dynamics, lowering costs, increasing profitability and improving service levels.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in international services, as the company optimizes its network.

evidence.text: We are continuing to execute our TNT Express integration plans and are scheduled to complete the integration of the FedEx Express and TNT Express linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations and begin offering an enhanced portfolio of international services in 2021. We expect to complete the final phase of international air network interoperability in early calendar 2022.

factors.summary: The company is actively integrating TNT Express, which is creating value by expanding capabilities and improving profitability in Europe.

evidence.text: We are continuing to execute our TNT Express integration plans and are scheduled to complete the integration of the FedEx Express and TNT Express linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations and begin offering an enhanced portfolio of international services in 2021. We expect the integration to improve our European revenue and profitability, which continue to underperform our expectations for that market.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as the expiration of the aviation excise tax holiday, are creating headwinds.

evidence.text: We expect headwinds related to the December 31, 2020 expiration of the aviation excise tax holiday created by the CARES Act and a higher effective income tax rate for the second half of 2021.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power across all transportation segments, with revenue growth driven by pricing initiatives. Despite lower fuel surcharges, yields have improved, reflecting effective pricing strategies.

evidence.text: International export package volume growth at FedEx Express and pricing initiatives across all of our transportation segments also contributed to the increase in revenue during the second quarter and first half of 2021.
FedEx Ground yields increased 7% in the second quarter and 5% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to yield improvement actions, partially offset by lower fuel surcharges.

Revenue was also positively impacted by pricing initiatives resulting from global air freight capacity constraints in the second quarter and first half of 2021. Composite freight yields increased 11% in both the second quarter and first half of 2021 primarily due to improved base yields.

factors.summary: Overall demand for delivery services is accelerating, driven by strong residential delivery demand due to the COVID-19 pandemic and growth in e-commerce. However, there is a noted decrease in business-to-business demand as restrictions have eased.

evidence.text: Elevated demand for our residential delivery services, resulting from the ongoing impact of the coronavirus (“COVID-19”) pandemic, continued during the second quarter of 2021.
FedEx Ground segment revenue increased 38% in the second quarter and 37% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to residential delivery volume growth.

The various governmental and other restrictions and slow down of commercial activities in major markets around the world has also led to unprecedented demand for residential delivery services, rivaling our peak holiday season traffic.
The demand for our U.S. domestic residential service offerings continued to accelerate during the second quarter of 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Organic growth is strong and accelerating, driven by volume increases and pricing initiatives across segments, particularly in FedEx Ground and FedEx Express. However, there are headwinds from weak global economic conditions and reduced business-to-business demand.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 19% in the second quarter and 16% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to volume growth in residential delivery services at FedEx Ground and U.S. domestic package volume growth at FedEx Express, both reflecting increased e-commerce demand resulting from the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.
FedEx Ground segment operating income increased 61% in the second quarter and 41% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to residential delivery volume growth and yield improvement.

FedEx Express segment revenue increased 14% in the second quarter and 11% in the first half of 2021 due to international export and U.S. domestic package volume growth, partially offset by lower fuel surcharges.

Continued weak global economic conditions reduced business-to-business demand for certain of our services in the first half of 2021. The various governmental and other restrictions and slow down of commercial activities in major markets around the world has also led to unprecedented demand for residential delivery services, rivaling our peak holiday season traffic.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments, particularly in residential delivery and package handling, driven by e-commerce demand. However, there is a noted decrease in business-to-business demand, which may impact the service mix and overall yields.

evidence.text: Volume growth contributed to a 25% increase in purchased transportation costs in the second quarter and a 24% increase in the first half of 2021, as well as an increase in salaries and employee benefits expense of 19% in the second quarter and 16% in the first half of 2021.
Average daily volume increased 29% in the second quarter and 30% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to continued growth in residential services driven by e-commerce.

During 2020, we incurred increased costs associated with this demand and lower composite yields than our typical service mix, and we continued to incur increased costs associated with this demand in the first half of 2021.

U.S. domestic package average daily volumes increased 18% in the second quarter and 13% in the first half of 2021 driven by growth in deferred service offerings, reflecting increased e-commerce demand resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic. International export package average daily volumes increased 19% in the second quarter and 17% in the first half of 2021 led by volume growth from Asia-Pacific and Europe.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed with overall improvement in gross and operating margins across several segments, driven by higher revenues and cost management, but also facing pressure from increased costs and operational adjustments due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Consolidated operating margin improved to 7.1% in the second quarter of 2021 from 3.2% in 2020, and to 7.7% in the first half of 2021 from 4.5% in 2020.
Operating margin increased to 7.5% in the second quarter and 9.6% in the first half of 2021, up from 6.4% and 9.4% respectively.
Operating margin for FedEx Express improved to 8.7% in the second quarter and 8.0% in the first half of 2021, up from 2.6% and 2.9% respectively, due to higher revenue and cost management.

The COVID-19 pandemic has also delayed completion of capital improvements and certain other initiatives in Europe related to the integration of TNT Express. These actions could result in increased costs to successfully implement our business strategy and effectively respond to changes in market dynamics, and could adversely affect our business and results of operations.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel prices, are decreasing, providing a benefit to operating income. However, lower fuel surcharges have partially offset the impact of decreased fuel prices. The company is also managing other input costs, such as labor and supply chain expenses, through pricing initiatives and operational efficiency improvements.

evidence.text: Fuel expense decreased 30% in the second quarter and 32% in the first half of 2021 due to lower fuel prices.
The net impact of fuel had a slightly negative impact to operating income in the second quarter and first half of 2021 as lower fuel surcharges outpaced decreased fuel prices.
Fuel costs decreased in the second quarter and first half of 2021, but the company is facing higher labor costs and supply chain expenses.
The company is offsetting some of these costs through pricing initiatives and operational efficiency.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction programs that are delivering measurable savings, particularly in integration expenses and margin improvements. However, these cost management actions, such as deferring capital projects, may hinder the execution of the business strategy.

evidence.text: We incurred integration expenses totaling $48 million in the second quarter and $97 million in the first half of 2021, a $16 million decrease from the second quarter and a $38 million decrease from the first half of 2020.

FedEx Freight segment operating income increased 79% in the second quarter and 57% in the first half of 2021 driven by continued focus on revenue quality initiatives, aligning our cost structure with current business levels and improving operational efficiencies.

Cost management and various cost-containment actions implemented across our business in response to the COVID-19 pandemic could hinder execution of our business strategy, including deferral of certain planned capital projects. Planned 2021 capital expenditures are below 2020 levels, as we have decreased planned spending on vehicles and trailers, delayed certain facility expansions and postponed certain information technology initiatives.

We expect the aggregate integration program expenses to be $1.7 billion through the completion of the physical network integration of TNT Express into FedEx Express in 2022. These actions are focused on reducing costs and improving efficiency.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising across various segments due to factors such as higher variable incentive compensation, wage inflation, and increased expenses related to COVID-19 safety measures. While the company is attempting to manage these costs through productivity improvements and network optimization, the overall impact on margins remains a concern.

evidence.text: Salaries and employee benefits expense increased 19% in the second quarter and 16% in the first half of 2021 due to higher variable incentive compensation expense and merit increases.

Salaries and employee benefits expense increased 60% in the second quarter and 54% in the first half of 2021 due to additional staffing to support volume growth, including costs associated with operating our seven-day network, merit increases and higher variable incentive compensation expense.

We have taken additional measures and are incurring increased operating expenses related to personal protective equipment and medical/safety supplies, as well as additional security and cleaning services in order to protect our team members and customers during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Salaries and employee benefits increased 15% in the second quarter and 13% in the first half of 2021, contributing to higher operating expenses. However, the company is optimizing its network to lower cost-to-serve.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable, supported by effective balance sheet management and strong liquidity positions.

evidence.text: With over $8.3 billion in cash and $3.8 billion in available liquidity under our $2.0 billion five-year credit agreement... we believe that our cash and cash equivalents, cash flow from operations and available financing sources will be adequate to meet internal and external liquidity needs.
Cash and cash equivalents totaled $8.3 billion at November 30, 2020, compared to $4.9 billion at May 31, 2020.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditures (capex) outlook is mixed. While there is a slight increase in expected capex from the previous estimate due to additional capacity initiatives, there are also notable decreases in spending across various segments such as vehicles, facilities, information technology, and aircraft. This suggests a cautious approach to investment despite some growth-oriented moves.

evidence.text: "Our capital expenditures are expected to be approximately $5.1 billion in 2021, a $0.8 billion decrease from 2020. The slight increase in our expected capital expenditures from the estimate in our Annual Report is due to capital investment for additional capacity initiatives in support of increased volumes."

"Capital expenditures decreased during the first half of 2021 primarily due to lower spending related to vehicles and facilities across all of our transportation segments, as well as decreased spending on information technology at FedEx Services and FedEx Freight, partially offset by increased spending on package handling equipment at FedEx Ground and higher spending related to aircraft at FedEx Express."

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing ongoing disruptions, primarily due to the pandemic, with constrained air capacity and increased costs impacting operations. While there are signs of improvement, such as strong demand for international shipments, the situation remains challenging with continued capacity constraints and elevated expenses.

evidence.text: Higher purchased transportation expenses at FedEx Ground are also driven by increased residential product mix in both the second quarter and first half of 2021. Additionally, higher variable incentive compensation expense negatively impacted year-over-year second quarter comparisons by approximately $215 million and first half comparisons by approximately $410 million.

The disruption of global supply chains, interruption in economic activity, preventative measures taken to alleviate the pandemic (such as governmental and other restrictions and other responsive measures), and increased economic uncertainty caused by the pandemic have resulted in increased global economic weakness of an unknown duration.

Government travel warnings as well as existing restrictions related to the COVID-19 pandemic are expected to continue to impact the demand for commercial air travel, thereby reducing available air freight capacity. As a result of these ongoing capacity constraints, we expect continued strong demand for international priority shipments for the remainder of 2021 to necessitate increased usage of our assets to support demand in key international supply chains.

factors.summary: The economic environment presents mixed conditions, with some areas benefiting from increased demand due to the pandemic, while others face challenges such as weaker demand and disruptions in the FedEx Freight and Express segments.

evidence.text: Elevated demand for our residential delivery services, resulting from the ongoing impact of the coronavirus ('COVID-19') pandemic, continued during the second quarter of 2021. In addition, demand for our business-to-business delivery services strengthened relative to the first quarter of 2021 as COVID-19-related restrictions moderated globally during the second quarter.

The demand for our service offerings in the first half of 2021 was negatively impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic and related supply chain disruptions, resulting in a 4% decrease in average daily shipments.

The COVID-19 pandemic has had certain adverse effects on our business, results of operations and financial condition, and we expect such adverse effects will continue. The COVID-19 pandemic has negatively impacted the global economy, disrupted global supply chains and created significant volatility and disruption of financial markets.

Our expectations for the remainder of 2021 are dependent on key external factors, including no further weakening of global economic conditions or additional shut-downs related to the COVID-19 pandemic, current fuel price expectations, and no additional adverse developments in international trade policies and relations.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting yields, particularly in international services, but the company is managing through pricing initiatives.

evidence.text: Our cash and cash equivalents balance at November 30, 2020 includes $1.9 billion of cash in foreign jurisdictions... We do not believe that the indefinite reinvestment of these funds impairs our ability to meet our U.S. domestic debt or working capital obligations.

International export package yields decreased 4% in the first half of 2021 primarily due to lower fuel surcharges and base yield declines driven by lower weight per package, partially offset by favorable exchange rates and pricing initiatives resulting from global air freight capacity constraints.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, economic conditions, and international trade policies, which could significantly impact near-term results.

evidence.text: the price and availability of jet and vehicle fuel;... the impact of foreign currency exchange rates, especially in the euro, Chinese yuan, British pound, Canadian dollar, Australian dollar and Mexican peso, which can affect our sales levels and foreign currency sales prices;
The net impact of fuel had a slightly negative impact to operating income in the second quarter and first half of 2021 as lower fuel surcharges outpaced decreased fuel prices.

Our expectations for the remainder of 2021 are dependent on key external factors, including no further weakening of global economic conditions or additional shut-downs related to the COVID-19 pandemic, current fuel price expectations, and no additional adverse developments in international trade policies and relations.

factors.summary: Management has consistently maintained forward guidance across multiple sections, reflecting confidence in the company's ability to manage liquidity and achieve growth despite challenges such as the COVID-19 pandemic and economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: We have and will continue to actively manage our cash flow... we believe that our cash and cash equivalents, cash flow from operations and available financing sources will be adequate to meet internal and external liquidity needs.

Cash flows from operating activities increased $3.2 billion in the first half of 2021 primarily due to higher net income and lower pension contributions. Capital expenditures decreased during the first half of 2021 primarily due to lower spending related to vehicles and facilities across all of our transportation segments.

Our expectations for the remainder of 2021 are dependent on key external factors, including no further weakening of global economic conditions or additional shut-downs related to the COVID-19 pandemic, current fuel price expectations, and no additional adverse developments in international trade policies and relations.

factors.summary: Management is showing mixed confidence signals. While they are increasing capital expenditures and maintaining liquidity, they are also cautiously extending surcharges and investing in network optimization, indicating some confidence in future growth despite cautious measures.

evidence.text: The company is increasing capital expenditures and maintaining liquidity through credit agreements and cash reserves.

In response to current business conditions, we are extending certain surcharges beyond the peak shipping period, although at a reduced rate. These surcharges will begin on January 18, 2021 and may be adjusted in future periods as business or market conditions evolve. We are continuing to execute our TNT Express integration plans and are scheduled to complete the integration of the FedEx Express and TNT Express linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations and begin offering an enhanced portfolio of international services in 2021.

factors.summary: The near-term risks are predominantly negative, driven by the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, including potential government restrictions, economic uncertainties, and increased competition. These factors create significant challenges and uncertainties for the company's operations and financial planning.

evidence.text: In response to current business and economic conditions as referenced above in the 'Outlook' section of this MD&A, we have and will continue to actively manage our cash flow, defer certain expenditures and seek to protect capital for unforeseen challenges from the ongoing pandemic.

The uncertainty concerning the COVID-19 pandemic, as well as the potential for additional government-related restrictions, continues to make any expectations for the second half of 2021 inherently less certain. We expect headwinds related to the December 31, 2020 expiration of the aviation excise tax holiday created by the CARES Act and a higher effective income tax rate for the second half of 2021.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident across multiple segments, driven by operational efficiency gains and technology investments, which are contributing to higher margins and cost reductions.

evidence.text: Salaries and employee benefits expense increased only 2% in the second quarter of 2021 primarily due to improved operational productivity, in spite of higher variable incentive compensation expense, merit increases and higher volumes.

We are focused on improving revenue quality and lowering costs through advanced technology aimed at improving productivity and safety. During the remainder of 2021, we will focus on last-mile residential delivery optimization, including by directing certain U.S. day-definite residential FedEx Express shipments into the FedEx Ground network to increase efficiency and lower our cost-to-serve.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction, focusing on investments in hubs and aircraft modernization, as well as the international expansion and integration of TNT Express to enhance profitability in Europe.

evidence.text: We expect these investments in hubs will provide productivity gains. We invested $1.3 billion in aircraft and related equipment in the first half of 2021 and expect to invest an additional $450 million for aircraft and related equipment during the remainder of 2021.

We are continuing to execute our TNT Express integration plans and are scheduled to complete the integration of the FedEx Express and TNT Express linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations and begin offering an enhanced portfolio of international services in 2021. We expect to incur additional costs, over multiple years, which may be material, including transformation costs and capital investments related to these actions.
